issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's expanded/collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1915: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_open = true;`. Line 1935: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger elements
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions in the dropdownMenu module show/hide dropdown panels without setting aria-expanded on their trigger buttons. This prevents screen reader users from knowing the dropdown state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2075: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.open = true;`. Line 2095: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState() function updates CSS classes for the billing period toggle but does not set aria-pressed on the toggle element. Screen reader users cannot perceive the toggle's pressed state (month/year), violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3265: inside the if (_toggleEl && _toggleTrack) block, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, isYear);` after `toggleClass(_toggleTrack, CSS.checked, isYear);`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter results update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters() function updates visible product cards and count badges but does not announce these changes via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users are not informed when filter results change, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2845: after `emit(_gridEl, 'filter:applied', { count: totalMatches, state: Object.assign({}, s) });`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : 'Filter applied, showing ' + totalMatches + ' products');`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult() function in inlineValidator adds error classes and messages but does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields. Screen reader users may not be alerted to field errors, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4335: in the else block (invalid case), add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);` after `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);`. In the if block (valid case), add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);` after `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialized on page load for dropdown triggers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on trigger buttons that start closed. Screen readers may not report the initial collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2060: after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: Click handler on non-button element without keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The _onToggleClick() function is attached to _toggleEl (a div with role="switch") but only handles click events. Keyboard users cannot activate the toggle with Enter or Space, violating WCAG 2.1.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3260: add a keydown event listener to _toggleEl that checks for Enter or Space and calls _onToggleClick. Example: `_toggleEl.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); _onToggleClick(); } });`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update (pricing period switch) not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod() function updates price displays and labels but does not announce the billing period change to screen readers. Users who rely on announcements may miss the update, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3250: after `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialized on page load for mobile nav trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the nav toggle button when the drawer is initially closed. Screen readers may not report the initial collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1955: after `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: Auto-playing carousel without pause control for reduced motion preference
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel.init() starts auto-play without checking prefersReducedMotion() first. Users who prefer reduced motion may be disrupted by auto-advancing slides, violating WCAG 2.2.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4645: change `if ((CONFIG.carousel ? CONFIG.carousel.autoPlay !== false : true) && !prefersReducedMotion()) {` to `if ((CONFIG.carousel ? CONFIG.carousel.autoPlay !== false : true) && !prefersReducedMotion()) {`.

fpTitles: Click handler on non-button element without keyboard support

issuesFound.title: Billing toggle switch missing aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle switch (role="switch") does not update the aria-checked attribute when toggled. Screen reader users will not hear the state change (monthly vs annual) and may not understand the current setting.
issuesFound.fix: In the _syncToggleState function, set aria-checked on the _toggleEl. Example: setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', isYear);

issuesFound.title: Product filter updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the grid updates to show matching products. This dynamic content change is not announced to screen reader users, who may not know that the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the grid, announce the number of results. Example: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field fails validation, the aria-invalid attribute is not set to true. Screen reader users may not be alerted that the field has an error.
issuesFound.fix: In the else branch of _renderResult, add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); and in the valid branch, set it to 'false'.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded set on page load. Screen reader users will not know the initial state (closed) of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: In the init function, set the initial state: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded set on page load. Screen reader users will not know the initial state (closed) of the dropdowns.
issuesFound.fix: In the register function, set the initial state: setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is opened, focus is not trapped inside the drawer. Keyboard users may tab out of the drawer and into the background, causing confusion.
issuesFound.fix: In the open function, create and activate a focus trap for the drawer. Example: _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer); _trap.activate();

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (selected by `[data-period]`) are not updated with `aria-pressed` to reflect their pressed state. Screen reader users cannot perceive which billing period is currently selected, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1046-1050: In the `_syncToggleState` function, add `btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', isActive ? 'true' : 'false');` after the `toggleClass` line.

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced via live region

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period switch lacks `aria‑checked` (or `aria‑pressed`) to convey state
issuesFound.explanation: The element with `data‑billing‑toggle` is given `role="switch"` for accessibility, but the script never sets the required state attribute (`aria‑checked` for a switch). Screen readers cannot announce whether the annual/monthly toggle is on or off. This violates the “SWEEP JS‑B” rule.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period buttons do not reflect selection with `aria‑pressed`
issuesFound.explanation: The period buttons (`data‑period="month"`, `data‑period="year"` …) are toggles that indicate which billing cycle is active, but the script only adds a CSS `.active` class. No `aria‑pressed` attribute is set, so assistive technology cannot convey which option is selected. This is another “SWEEP JS‑B” violation.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not set `aria‑invalid` on error fields (and does not clear it on success)
Mobile navigation trigger missing initial aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: During mobileNav.init() the trigger button is never given an initial aria‑expanded value, leaving its state undefined for screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria‑expanded when panel opens/closes
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module toggles panels but never sets aria‑expanded on the associated button, violating the ARIA disclosure pattern.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing initial aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is registered, the trigger button is not given an initial aria‑expanded value, so its collapsed state is not conveyed to AT.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search combobox does not update aria‑expanded when suggestions list opens/closes
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch module shows and hides the suggestion list but never toggles aria‑expanded on the search <input>, so screen readers cannot know when the list is visible.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input missing initial aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: During siteSearch.init() the search <input> is never given an initial aria‑expanded value, leaving its collapsed state undefined.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle buttons do not reflect state via aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module adds an “active” CSS class to the period buttons but never updates aria‑pressed, which is required for toggle controls.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle buttons missing initial aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: When the pricing module initializes, the period buttons are not given an initial aria‑pressed value, so their default state is ambiguous to AT.

issuesFound.title: Product‑view‑mode toggle buttons do not update aria‑pressed when mode changes
issuesFound.explanation: The view‑mode buttons toggle the grid/list layout but only toggle a CSS class; they never set aria‑pressed, leaving the toggle’s state invisible to AT.

issuesFound.title: Product‑view‑mode toggle buttons missing initial aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: During productFilter.init() the view‑mode buttons are not given an initial aria‑pressed value, so their default state is not conveyed to AT.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav toggle button (`_trigger`) never receives an `aria‑expanded` attribute. It is not set to “false” on page load and not updated to “true”/“false” when the menu opens or closes, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not reflect errors via aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` renders error messages but never sets `aria‑invalid="true"` on invalid fields nor removes it when the field becomes valid. Screen readers therefore do not receive the error state.

issuesFound.title: Pricing “annual billing” switch lacks aria‑checked and is not updated
issuesFound.explanation: The simple toggle (`_toggleEl`) is given `role="switch"` but never receives the required `aria‑checked` state, nor is the attribute updated when the user switches between monthly and annual billing. This violates ARIA switch design patterns.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not expose state via aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The period selection buttons (`_periodBtns`) are toggled visually with a CSS class but never receive `aria‑pressed` to convey their selected state to assistive technologies.

Line 375: `

Connect your project

`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `:focus-visible` rule removes the default focus outline with `outline: none` but does not provide a visible alternative on the base `:focus` selector. Keyboard users lose focus visibility, violating WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible (Level AA).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1036: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`
    Line 1040: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-sm` selector sets `min-height: 20px`, which is below the 24×24 px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum) (Level AA). Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate small buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1100: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img` selector applies a `transform` animation on hover (`rotateY(-3deg) rotateX(1deg)` to `rotateY(0deg) rotateX(0deg)`) but the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block does not override or disable this motion. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience unwanted animation, violating WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions (Level AA).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1209: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img, .hero-image img:hover { transform: none !important; } }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible rule at line 1052 removes the default focus outline with `outline: none` but does not provide a visible alternative for keyboard users on all interactive elements. This affects users who rely on keyboard navigation, as they will not see a focus indicator, making it difficult or impossible to track their position on the page.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-sm` selector sets a `min-height: 20px` which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 24×24 pixels. This affects users with motor impairments or those using touch devices, as the small target size makes it difficult to accurately activate the button.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img` selector has a transform animation on hover that moves the image. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable or slow this motion. This affects users who are sensitive to motion, potentially causing discomfort or nausea.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which reduces the space between words. This can impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision, as it makes text harder to distinguish and read.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `letter-spacing: -0.05em`, which reduces the space between letters. This can impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision, as it makes text harder to distinguish and read.

issuesFound.title: Font size below 16px for body text
issuesFound.explanation: The `html` selector sets `font-size: 12px`, which is below the recommended minimum of 16px for body text. This affects users with low vision or reading difficulties, as smaller text is harder to read.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The `a` selector sets `text-decoration: none`, removing underlines from links. There is no non-colour visual differentiator (e.g., font-weight, border-bottom, background highlight) provided on hover or focus. This affects users with low vision or colour blindness, as they may not be able to distinguish links from surrounding text.

fpTitles: Font size below 16px for body text

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `:focus:not(:focus-visible)` removes the default focus outline without providing a visible alternative for keyboard users who rely on focus indicators to navigate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1020: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The `.nav-toggle` selector sets a `min-height` and `min-width` of `28px`, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 24×24 px (44×44 px recommended). Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate this control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1108: `.nav-toggle { min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` utility class incorrectly uses `position: static` and does not hide content from the accessibility tree. It should use `position: absolute`, `clip`, and small dimensions to hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img:hover` selector applies a `transform` animation on hover, but there is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable or slow this motion. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience discomfort or distraction.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The `a { text-decoration: none; }` rule removes underlines from all links, and no alternative non-colour visual differentiator (e.g., font-weight, border-bottom, background highlight) is provided on `:hover` or `:focus`. This violates WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color, as colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 300: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` or provide an alternative visual cue on hover/focus, e.g.:
  `a:hover, a:focus { text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle (`.nav-toggle`) is a `<div>` styled as a button but lacks keyboard event handlers for Enter/Space. Users who rely on keyboard navigation cannot open/close the mobile menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12345: Replace the `<div>` with a native `<button>` element, or add `role="button"`, `tabindex="0"`, and keydown handlers for Enter/Space in the `mobileNav` module.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` and `mobileNav.close()` functions toggle the mobile menu's visibility but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`.nav-toggle`). Screen readers cannot announce the menu's open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12378: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` in `open()` and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in `close()`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle (`.pricing-toggle`) is a `<div>` styled as a toggle button but lacks the `aria-pressed` attribute to indicate its pressed state. Screen readers cannot announce whether the toggle is on/off.
issuesFound.fix: Line 11802: Replace the `<div>` with a native `<button>` element, add `role="switch"`, and update `aria-pressed` in the `_onToggleClick()` handler.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing.setPeriod()` function updates the displayed price and savings message but does not announce these changes to screen readers via an ARIA live region.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 11850: Add a live region element (e.g., `
`) and update its text content in `setPeriod()` to announce the new billing period and savings.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator.validate()` function marks invalid fields with an error class but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input elements. Screen readers cannot announce that the field is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 14602: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` in the error branch of `_renderResult()`.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator.validate()` function clears the error state but does not reset `aria-invalid` to `false` or remove the attribute on valid inputs. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 14602: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` in the valid branch of `_renderResult()`.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger (`.nav-toggle`) does not have `aria-expanded="false"` set on page load, so screen readers cannot announce that the menu is initially closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12360: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in the `mobileNav.init()` function.

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for search input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input (`#search-input`) lacks an accessible name. Screen readers announce it as "edit text" without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12605: Add `aria-label="Search"` or associate it with a `<label>` element.

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for newsletter email input
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input (`#newsletter-email`) lacks an accessible name. Screen readers announce it as "edit text" without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 14705: Add `aria-label="Email address"` or associate it with a `<label>` element.

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for contact form inputs
issuesFound.explanation: Contact form inputs (`#contact-first-name`, `#contact-last-name`, `#contact-email`, `#contact-message`) lack accessible names. Screen readers announce them as "edit text" without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 14805: Add `<label>` elements for each input or use `aria-label` attributes (e.g., `aria-label="First name"`).

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for file upload zone
issuesFound.explanation: The file upload zone (`.upload-zone`) lacks an accessible name. Screen readers announce it as "button" without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 15012: Add `aria-label="Upload files"` or use a visually hidden `<label>` element.

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for cookie consent buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie consent "Accept" and "Decline" buttons lack accessible names. Screen readers announce them as "button" without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 15805: Add `aria-label="Accept all cookies"` and `aria-label="Decline non-essential cookies"` to the respective buttons.

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name for theme toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Theme toggle buttons (e.g., `[data-theme-toggle="light"]`) lack accessible names. Screen readers announce them as "button" without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 16205: Add `aria-label="Switch to light theme"`, `aria-label="Switch to dark theme"`, and `aria-label="Use system theme"` to the respective buttons.

issuesFound.title: Broken aria-controls reference
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu trigger (`#nav-menu > li > button[aria-haspopup]`) references a non-existent `aria-controls` target. The `aria-controls` attribute points to an ID that does not exist in the DOM.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12450: Ensure the `aria-controls` attribute on the trigger matches the `id` of the dropdown panel (e.g., `aria-controls="dropdown-1-submenu"`).

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute for personal data inputs
issuesFound.explanation: Contact form inputs (`#contact-first-name`, `#contact-last-name`, `#contact-email`) collect personal data but lack the `autocomplete` attribute. This prevents browsers from offering autofill suggestions, reducing usability and accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 14805: Add `autocomplete="given-name"` to `#contact-first-name`, `autocomplete="family-name"` to `#contact-last-name`, and `autocomplete="email"` to `#contact-email`.

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute for personal data input
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input (`#newsletter-email`) collects personal data but lacks the `autocomplete` attribute. This prevents browsers from offering autofill suggestions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 14705: Add `autocomplete="email"` to the input.

issuesFound.title: Missing legend for fieldset
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter consent checkbox is wrapped in a `<fieldset>` but lacks a `<legend>` element. Screen readers cannot announce the purpose of the group.
issuesFound.fix: Line 14710: Add `<legend>Subscribe to newsletter</legend>` to the `<fieldset>`.

issuesFound.title: Missing scope attribute for table headers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing comparison table (`<table>`) contains `<th>` elements without `scope="col"` or `scope="row"` attributes. Screen readers cannot associate headers with their respective data cells.
issuesFound.fix: Line 11905: Add `scope="col"` to column header `<th>` elements and `scope="row"` to row header `<th>` elements in the table.

issuesFound.title: Missing alt text for decorative images
issuesFound.explanation: Decorative images (e.g., ``) lack `alt=""` attributes. Screen readers may announce the filename or "image" unnecessarily.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10505: Add `alt=""` to all decorative images.

issuesFound.title: Missing alt text for informative images
issuesFound.explanation: Informative images (e.g., ``) lack descriptive `alt` text. Screen reader users miss important content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10510: Add descriptive `alt` text (e.g., `alt="Screenshot of Luminary Studio dashboard showing project management features"`).

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label for social media links
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links (`.social-links a`) lack accessible names. Screen readers announce them as "link" without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10905: Add `aria-label="Twitter"`, `aria-label="GitHub"`, etc., to each social media link.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label for dismiss buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Toast notification dismiss buttons (`.toast__dismiss`) lack accessible names. Screen readers announce them as "button" without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 11205: Add `aria-label="Dismiss notification"` to each dismiss button.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label for scroll-to-top button
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button (`#scroll-top-btn`) lacks an accessible name. Screen readers announce it as "button" without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12505: Add `aria-label="Scroll to top"` to the button.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label for carousel navigation buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Carousel navigation buttons (`.carousel-prev`, `.carousel-next`) lack accessible names. Screen readers announce them as "button" without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 14205: Add `aria-label="Previous testimonial"` and `aria-label="Next testimonial"` to the respective buttons.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label for file upload remove buttons
issuesFound.explanation: File upload remove buttons (`.upload-entry__remove`) lack accessible names. Screen readers announce them as "button" without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 15050: Add `aria-label="Remove file"` to each remove button.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label for wizard navigation buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Wizard navigation buttons (e.g., `[data-wizard-prev]`) lack accessible names. Screen readers announce them as "button" without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 14905: Add `aria-label="Back to previous step"` and `aria-label="Continue to next step"` to the respective buttons.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-live region for dynamic form status
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form status message (`.newsletter-status`) is not announced to screen readers when updated. Users are not informed of success/error states.
issuesFound.fix: Line 14750: Add `aria-live="polite"` to the status element.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-live region for dynamic filter status
issuesFound.explanation: The product filter count badge (`[data-product-count]`) is not announced to screen readers when updated. Users are not informed of the number of matching products.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12805: Add `aria-live="polite"` to the count badge element.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-live region for dynamic pricing status
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing savings message (`[data-savings-message]`) is not announced to screen readers when updated. Users are not informed of the savings amount.
issuesFound.fix: Line 11850: Add `aria-live="polite"` to the savings message element.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-live region for dynamic FAQ status
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion does not announce the expanded/collapsed state of items to screen readers. Users are not informed of the change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 13589: Add a live region element (e.g., `
`) and update its text content in `toggle()` to announce the state change.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-live region for dynamic carousel status
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonials carousel does not announce the current slide to screen readers. Users are not informed of the slide change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 14250: Add a live region element (e.g., `
`) and update its text content in `goTo()` to announce the current slide.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-atomic for live regions
issuesFound.explanation: Toast notification live regions (`role="alert"`) lack `aria-atomic="true"`. Screen readers may not announce the entire message if only part of it changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 11205: Add `aria-atomic="true"` to all live region elements.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-relevant for live regions
issuesFound.explanation: The live region announcer (`#luminary-live-police`, `#luminary-live-assertive`) lacks `aria-relevant="additions text"`. Screen readers may not announce changes if the text is updated rather than added.
issuesFound.fix: Line 11505: Add `aria-relevant="additions text"` to both live region elements.

issuesFound.title: Missing role for progress bar
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard progress bar (`[data-wizard-progress]`) lacks `role="progressbar"`. Screen readers cannot announce it as a progress indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 14950: Add `role="progressbar"` to the progress bar element.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-valuemin/aria-valuemax for progress bar
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard progress bar (`[data-wizard-progress]`) lacks `aria-valuemin` and `aria-valuemax` attributes. Screen readers cannot announce the progress range.
issuesFound.fix: Line 14950: Add `aria-valuemin="0"` and `aria-valuemax="100"` to the progress bar element.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-valuenow for progress bar
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard progress bar (`[data-wizard-progress]`) lacks `aria-valuenow`. Screen readers cannot announce the current progress value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 14950: Add `aria-valuenow="0"` to the progress bar element and update it in `_updateProgress()`.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-valuetext for progress bar
issuesFound.explanation: The wizard progress bar (`[data-wizard-progress]`) lacks `aria-valuetext`. Screen readers cannot announce a human-readable progress description.
issuesFound.fix: Line 14950: Add `aria-valuetext="Step 1 of 3"` to the progress bar element and update it in `_updateProgress()`.

issuesFound.title: Missing role for tab list
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu navigation (`#nav-menu`) lacks `role="menubar"` or `role="tablist"`. Screen readers cannot announce it as a navigation landmark.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12401: Add `role="menubar"` to the navigation element.

issuesFound.title: Missing role for tab
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu items (`#nav-menu > li > button[aria-haspopup]`) lack `role="menuitem"` or `role="tab"`. Screen readers cannot announce them as interactive menu items.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12401: Add `role="menuitem"` to each dropdown trigger button.

issuesFound.title: Missing role for tab panel
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu panels (`#nav-menu [id$="-submenu"]`) lack `role="menu"` or `role="tabpanel"`. Screen readers cannot announce them as menu containers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12401: Add `role="menu"` to each dropdown panel.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-selected for tabs
issuesFound.explanation: Wizard step navigation buttons (e.g., `[data-wizard-prev]`) lack `aria-selected`. Screen readers cannot announce which tab is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 14905: Add `aria-selected="false"` to all navigation buttons and update it to `true` for the active step.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-controls for tabs
issuesFound.explanation: Wizard step navigation buttons (e.g., `[data-wizard-prev]`) lack `aria-controls`. Screen readers cannot associate tabs with their panels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 14905: Add `aria-controls="step-1"` (matching the panel `id`) to each navigation button.

issuesFound.title: Missing role for combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search input (`#search-input`) lacks `role="combobox"`. Screen readers cannot announce it as a combobox widget.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12605: Add `role="combobox"` to the search input.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-expanded for combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search input (`#search-input`) lacks `aria-expanded`. Screen readers cannot announce whether the suggestion list is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12605: Add `aria-expanded="false"` to the search input and update it in `siteSearch._open()` and `siteSearch._close()`.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-autocomplete for combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search input (`#search-input`) lacks `aria-autocomplete`. Screen readers cannot announce the autocomplete behavior.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12605: Add `aria-autocomplete="list"` to the search input.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-controls for combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search input (`#search-input`) lacks `aria-controls`. Screen readers cannot associate the combobox with its suggestion list.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12605: Add `aria-controls="search-suggestions"` (matching the list `id`) to the search input.

issuesFound.title: Missing role for listbox
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion list (`#search-suggestions`) lacks `role="listbox"`. Screen readers cannot announce it as a list of options.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12650: Add `role="listbox"` to the suggestion list.

issuesFound.title: Missing role for option
issuesFound.explanation: Search suggestion items (`#search-suggestions li`) lack `role="option"`. Screen readers cannot announce them as selectable options.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12650: Add `role="option"` to each suggestion list item.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-selected for options
issuesFound.explanation: Search suggestion items (`#search-suggestions li`) lack `aria-selected`. Screen readers cannot announce which option is highlighted.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12650: Add `aria-selected="false"` to all suggestion items and update it in `suggestionListRenderer.setActiveIndex()`.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-activedescendant for combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search input (`#search-input`) lacks `aria-activedescendant`. Screen readers cannot announce which suggestion is highlighted.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12605: Add `aria-activedescendant="suggestion-item-0"` (matching the active item `id`) to the search input and update it in `suggestionListRenderer.setActiveIndex()`.

issuesFound.title: Missing role for list
issuesFound.explanation: The file upload list (`.upload-list`) lacks `role="list"`. Screen readers cannot announce it as a list of files.
issuesFound.fix: Line 15050: Add `role="list"` to the upload list element.

issuesFound.title: Missing role for listitem
issuesFound.explanation: File upload list items (`.upload-entry`) lack `role="listitem"`. Screen readers cannot announce them as list items.
issuesFound.fix: Line 15050: Add `role="listitem"` to each upload entry element.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label for progress bar
issuesFound.explanation: File upload progress bars (`.upload-entry__progress`) lack `aria-label`. Screen readers cannot announce their purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 15050: Add `aria-label="Upload progress for {filename}"` to each progress bar.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-busy for loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing section does not use `aria-busy` during price animations. Screen readers cannot announce that the content is loading.
issuesFound.fix: Line 11850: Add `aria-busy="true"` to the pricing section during animations and `aria-busy="false"` when complete.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label for landmark regions
issuesFound.explanation: The `<header>` element lacks `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`. Screen readers cannot distinguish it from other headers on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10105: Add `aria-label="Site header"` to the `<header>` element.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label for search landmark
issuesFound.explanation: The search form (`#site-search`) lacks `role="search"` or `aria-label`. Screen readers cannot announce it as a search landmark.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12605: Add `role="search"` and `aria-label="Site search"` to the search form.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label for complementary landmark
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter section (`.newsletter-section`) lacks `role="complementary"` or `aria-label`. Screen readers cannot announce it as a complementary landmark.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10605: Add `role="complementary"` and `aria-label="Newsletter signup"` to the newsletter section.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label for region landmark
issuesFound.explanation: The contact section (`.contact-section`) lacks `role="region"` or `aria-label`. Screen readers cannot announce it as a distinct region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10705: Add `role="region"` and `aria-label="Contact us"` to the contact section.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label for banner landmark
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie consent banner lacks `role="banner"` or `aria-label`. Screen readers cannot announce it as a banner landmark.
issuesFound.fix: Line 15805: Add `role="banner"` and `aria-label="Cookie consent"` to the cookie banner element.

issuesFound.title: Missing skip link
issuesFound.explanation: The page lacks a skip link to bypass repetitive navigation. Keyboard users must tab through all navigation links on every page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10105: Add `Skip to main content` as the first focusable element in the ``.

issuesFound.title: Missing heading hierarchy
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section (`.hero`) lacks an `<h1>` heading. The page has no top-level heading, which is essential for screen reader navigation.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10505: Add `

Luminary Studio

` or similar to the hero section.

issuesFound.title: Missing lang attribute for page
issuesFound.explanation: The `<html>` element lacks a `lang` attribute. Screen readers cannot announce the page's language, leading to incorrect pronunciation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1: Add `lang="en"` to the `<html>` element.

issuesFound.title: Missing title for page
issuesFound.explanation: The `<title>` element is missing or empty. Screen readers cannot announce the page's title, and browser tabs lack context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1: Add `<title>Luminary Studio | Modern Design Tools</title>` or similar to the `<head>`.

issuesFound.title: Missing focus indicator for interactive elements
issuesFound.explanation: Interactive elements (buttons, links, form inputs) lack visible focus indicators. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10105: Add `:focus-visible` styles to all interactive elements, or use the `keyboardNavigationGuard` to apply `data-navigation="keyboard"` and style accordingly.

issuesFound.title: Missing focus trap for modal dialog
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie consent banner does not trap keyboard focus. Keyboard users can tab out of the banner, losing context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 15805: Use `createFocusTrap()` to trap focus within the banner while it is open.

issuesFound.title: Missing focus return for modal dialog
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie consent banner does not return focus to the triggering element when closed. Keyboard users lose their place in the tab order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 15805: Store the previously focused element when opening the banner and return focus to it when closed.

issuesFound.title: Missing reduced motion guard for animations
issuesFound.explanation: Price animations do not respect `prefers-reduced-motion`. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience discomfort.
issuesFound.fix: Line 11850: Wrap price animations in `if (!prefersReducedMotion()) { ... }` or use `animateNumber()` with reduced-motion support.

issuesFound.title: Missing touch target size for interactive elements
issuesFound.explanation: Interactive elements (buttons, links) have touch targets smaller than 44×44 pixels. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate them.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10105: Ensure all interactive elements have a minimum touch target size of 44×44 pixels using padding or `min-width`/`min-height`.

issuesFound.title: Decorative icon missing aria-hidden={true}
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron icon inside the NavDropdown trigger button is decorative but not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers will announce the arrow characters, which is redundant since the button's accessible name already conveys its state via aria-expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1502: `<span aria-hidden="true" className="nav-link__chevron">`

issuesFound.title: Required-field marker announces bare asterisk to AT — add aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The required-field marker (*) in FormGroup is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers will announce the asterisk as text, which is redundant since the "required" state is already conveyed via the required attribute and the visually hidden "(required)" text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 942: ` *`

issuesFound.title: Toggle component trigger missing aria-expanded prop
issuesFound.explanation: The NavDropdown trigger button does not receive an aria-expanded prop to reflect the isOpen state. Screen readers cannot announce whether the dropdown is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1499: `<button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>`

issuesFound.title: Form field missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component in SearchInput does not have an explicit accessible label. While it has an aria-label="Search", this is not sufficient because the label is not programmatically associated with the input. The label element exists but is visually hidden, so it should use htmlFor to associate with the input.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1003: `<TextInput id={id} type="search" value={value} onChange={(ev) => onChange(ev.target.value)} placeholder={placeholder} autoComplete="off" aria-label="Search" className="site-search-form__input" />`
  Fix: Ensure the label element at line 999 has `htmlFor={id}`.

issuesFound.title: Form field missing aria-invalid state
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component in NewsletterForm does not receive an aria-invalid prop when the email field is in an error state. Screen readers cannot announce that the field is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1130: `<TextInput id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" placeholder="you@company.com" autoComplete="email" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} required invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} aria-invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} />`

issuesFound.title: Interactive state not communicated to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The FilterTabs buttons do not receive an aria-pressed prop to reflect the activeFilter state. Screen readers cannot announce which filter tab is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1450: `<button type="button" className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}>`

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: The submit button in NewsletterForm does not receive an aria-busy prop when the form is submitting. Screen readers cannot announce that the button is in a loading state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1145: `<Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" loading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-busy={isSubmitting} aria-describedby={statusId}>`

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current navigation link in DesktopNav does not receive an aria-current="page" prop. Screen readers cannot announce which link represents the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1408: `<a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: Spinner icon missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The spinner icon inside the Button component is not hidden from assistive technology. The visually hidden loading label already conveys the loading state, so the spinner should be hidden to avoid redundancy.
issuesFound.fix: Line 880: `<span aria-hidden="true" className="btn-spinner" />`

issuesFound.title: Required field not programmatically marked as required
issuesFound.explanation: The name field in ContactForm is marked as required visually but does not have the required attribute or aria-required prop. Screen readers cannot announce that the field is required.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1220: `<TextInput id="contact-name" name="name" type="text" autoComplete="name" required aria-required="true" value={name.state.value} onChange={(ev) => name.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={name.onBlur} invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error} aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name-error' : undefined} />`

issuesFound.title: Form field error message not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The error message for the email field in NewsletterForm is not connected to the TextInput via aria-describedby when the field is in an error state. Screen readers cannot announce the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1130: `<TextInput id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" placeholder="you@company.com" autoComplete="email" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} required invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} aria-invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} />`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed globally without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without defining a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This removes the focus ring for all interactive elements that do not have a specific override, making keyboard navigation impossible to track.
issuesFound.fix: Line 533: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on buttons without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule explicitly sets outline: none but does not provide a visible box-shadow or border. Combined with the global reset, buttons lose their focus indication entirely for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 663: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on feature card links without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Users navigating by keyboard cannot identify which card link is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1225: .feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on footer navigation links without replacement

issuesFound.fix: Line 1763: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-neutral-0); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on social links without replacement

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and clip: auto, which renders content visibly on the page instead of hiding it from sighted users while keeping it for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 563: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on feature card text
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card p text uses #aaaaaa (--colour-text-secondary) on a #ffffff background. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.85:1, which fails the WCAG 2.2 AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1133: .feature-card p { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-700); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); margin: 0; flex-grow: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer brand text
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand > p text uses #94a3b8 (--colour-neutral-400) on a #555555 (--colour-neutral-900) background. The contrast ratio is approximately 3.2:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1753: .footer-brand > p { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-100); line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed); margin: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer bottom text
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-bottom > p text uses #64748b (--colour-neutral-500) on a #555555 background. The contrast ratio is approximately 1.2:1, making the text nearly illegible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1793: .footer-bottom > p { font-size: var(--font-size-xs); color: var(--colour-neutral-200); margin: 0; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img element has a transform transition defined, but the corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at Line 1033 is empty and does not disable the animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1033: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation on buttons missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn element has a transform transition defined for hover/active states. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule that disables this specific motion for buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 633: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target size below minimum for icon buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px. WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.8 requires a minimum target size of 24x24 pixels (AA) or 44x44 pixels (AAA).
issuesFound.fix: Line 723: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target size below minimum for checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] is explicitly sized to 20x20 pixels. This is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24x24 pixels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1713: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing can cause words to overlap or become illegible for users with cognitive disabilities or when text spacing is adjusted.

issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none. Links in the body text rely solely on color to distinguish them from surrounding text, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 if color perception is impaired.

issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have text-decoration: none and rely on color change for hover state. Without an underline or other non-color indicator, they may not be identifiable as links.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation menu opens or closes, no announcement is made to the live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the state change unless they manually check the menu status.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens or closes, no announcement is made to the live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the state change.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open function sets the panel to visible but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched (e.g., monthly to annual), the change is not announced to the live region. Screen reader users may not realize the prices have updated.

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The product count badge text is updated when filters are applied, but the change is not announced to the live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the new result count.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function associates the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Assistive technologies rely on aria-invalid to identify invalid inputs.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open() and close() functions modify the drawer visibility and CSS classes but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users are not informed whether the navigation menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1715: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);
  Line 1745: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open() and close() functions change the visible state of the navigation menu but do not announce this change via a live region. Screen reader users are not notified when the menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1715: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');
  Line 1745: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element

Line 1920: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions change the visible state of the dropdown panel but do not announce this change via a live region. Screen reader users are not notified of the state change.

issuesFound.title: Fieldset missing legend
issuesFound.explanation: The fieldset grouping the interest checkboxes lacks a legend element, failing to provide a programmatic label that describes the purpose of the group.
issuesFound.fix: Line 617: <fieldset><legend>Interests</legend>

issuesFound.title: Missing language declaration
issuesFound.explanation: The html element lacks a lang attribute, preventing screen readers from applying correct pronunciation rules. This fails WCAG 3.1.1 Language of Page.

issuesFound.title: Image missing alt attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The hero dashboard image has no alt attribute, leaving screen reader users without a description of the visual content. This fails WCAG 1.1.1 Non-text Content.

issuesFound.title: Non-descriptive link text
issuesFound.explanation: The link text "Read more" does not describe the destination or purpose of the link when taken out of context. Screen reader users navigating by links list will not understand where the link leads. This fails WCAG 2.4.4 Link Purpose (In Context).

issuesFound.explanation: The document structure jumps from an h2 heading to an h5 heading without intermediate h3 and h4 levels, creating an illogical outline for screen reader users navigating by headings. This fails WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships.

Connect your project

issuesFound.title: Table header missing scope

issuesFound.explanation: The first name input collects personal information but lacks an autocomplete attribute, preventing browsers from assisting users with autofill and preventing assistive technologies from understanding the input purpose. This fails WCAG 1.3.5 Identify Input Purpose.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class removes the default outline on :focus-visible but does not provide a visible alternative focus indicator such as a box-shadow or border-color change. Keyboard users cannot see which button has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 726: Add a visible focus indicator, for example: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: var(--shadow-focus); }` or add `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);` to the existing rule.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link removes outline on :focus-visible without providing an alternative visual indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when the logo link is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 837: Add a visible focus indicator such as `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);` or `outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);`.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes outline on :focus-visible without providing an alternative visual indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when the toggle is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 857: Add a visible focus indicator such as `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);`.

issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes outline on :focus-visible without providing an alternative visual indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when the button is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 649: Add a visible focus indicator such as `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);`.

issuesFound.fix: Line 672: Add a visible focus indicator such as `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);` or `text-decoration: underline;`.

issuesFound.fix: Line 911: Add a visible focus indicator such as `background-color: var(--colour-info-light);` or `box-shadow: inset 0 0 0 2px var(--colour-brand-primary);`.

issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links remove outline on :focus-visible without providing an alternative visual indicator. Keyboard users cannot see which card link is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 949: Add a visible focus indicator such as `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);`.

issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons remove outline on :focus-visible without providing an alternative visual indicator. Keyboard users cannot see which filter button is focused.

issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons remove outline on :focus-visible without providing an alternative visual indicator. Keyboard users cannot see which billing option is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1164: Add a visible focus indicator such as `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);`.

issuesFound.fix: Line 1258: Add a visible focus indicator such as `border: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary);` or `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);`.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove outline on :focus-visible without providing an alternative visual indicator. Keyboard users cannot see which FAQ item is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1370: Add a visible focus indicator such as `box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring);` or `background-color: var(--colour-neutral-100);`.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs have outline removed on :focus-visible (via global rule and specific selector) but lack a replacement visual indicator such as a border-color change or box-shadow. Keyboard users cannot see when checkboxes are focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1413: Add a visible focus indicator such as `.form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px; }`.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has a min-height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 802: Increase minimum height to at least 24px: `min-height: 24px;` (or preferably 44px for comfortable tapping).

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons have min-width and min-height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these small buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 814: Increase minimum dimensions to at least 24px: `min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px;` (or preferably 44px).

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes have explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty selecting these checkboxes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1413: Increase dimensions to at least 24px: `width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px;` and consider adding padding or a larger hit area via a label wrapper.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class broken
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which fails to visually hide content. Content intended only for screen readers will be visible to all users, breaking the layout and design. Additionally, .not-sr-only appears identical to .sr-only, which is confusing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 476-506: Replace with a proper visually-hidden pattern: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }`.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons have transition properties including transform that animate on user interaction, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable these animations for users who experience vestibular disorders or motion sensitivity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 713-718: Add reduced motion support: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }`.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: Skip links have a transform transition that animates when the link receives focus, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 411: Add reduced motion support: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }`.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button has transition properties for background-color and border-color, but if these are considered motion that could affect users, they should be disabled. More importantly, any future transform transitions should be guarded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 852: Add reduced motion support: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .nav-toggle { transition: none; } }`.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on #nav-menu > li > a and button
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation menu items have transition properties for color and background-color. While less problematic than transform, these should ideally be disabled for users requesting reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 882: Add reduced motion support: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button { transition: none; } }`.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links have transition properties that animate on hover/focus, but lack a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 905: Add reduced motion support: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { [id$="-submenu"] li a { transition: none; } }`.

issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has a color transition that animates on hover, but lacks a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 953: Add reduced motion support: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { #site-search button[type="submit"] { transition: none; } }`.

issuesFound.fix: Line 968: Add reduced motion support: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a { transition: none; } }`.

issuesFound.explanation: Hero CTA buttons have transition properties including transform (translateY) on hover, but lack a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1001-1005: Add reduced motion support: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: none; } }`.

issuesFound.explanation: Secondary hero buttons have border-color and color transitions, but lack a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override.

issuesFound.fix: Line 1021: Add reduced motion support: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .logo-list img { transition: none; } }`.

issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons have transition properties for background-color, color, and box-shadow, but lack a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override.

issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons have transition properties for multiple properties, but lack a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1156-1160: Add reduced motion support: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-toggle button { transition: none; } }`.

issuesFound.explanation: Testimonial cards have box-shadow and border-color transitions, but lack a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override.

issuesFound.explanation: FAQ buttons have background-color and color transitions (in addition to the ::after transition which is covered), but lack a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override for the button itself.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1359-1360: Add reduced motion support: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .faq-item dt button { transition: none; } }`.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 271: Remove negative word-spacing: `word-spacing: normal;` or use `word-spacing: 0;`.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has letter-spacing: -0.05em, which tightens character spacing and can reduce readability for users with visual impairments or dyslexia.
issuesFound.fix: Line 272: Remove negative letter-spacing: `letter-spacing: normal;` or use `letter-spacing: 0;`.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-danger
issuesFound.explanation: The danger button uses background-color #ee9999 (light red) with white text (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.6:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text and 3:1 for large text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 776: Use a darker background color: `background-color: var(--colour-error-dark);` (#991b1b) which provides 12.5:1 contrast, or adjust the existing --colour-error variable to a darker shade.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on code elements
issuesFound.explanation: Inline code elements use color #bb88ee (light purple) on background #f1f5f9 (light grey), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.6:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 298: Use a darker color for code text: `color: var(--colour-brand-secondary-hover);` (#581c87) or `color: var(--colour-neutral-800);` (#1e293b).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .step-number
issuesFound.explanation: Step numbers use white text on a gradient background including #bb88ee (light purple). White on light purple results in approximately 1.9:1 contrast ratio, failing WCAG AA requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 961: Use a darker text color such as `color: var(--colour-neutral-900);` or darken the gradient background colors.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .stat-label
issuesFound.explanation: Statistics labels use rgba(255,255,255,0.3) (white at 30% opacity) on a background of approximately #5588bb (blue), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.1:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1137: Increase opacity for better contrast: `color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9);` or use `color: var(--colour-neutral-0);` (white) with no opacity reduction.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .newsletter-section
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter section uses white text on a gradient background from #5588bb to #bb88ee. On the light purple end (#bb88ee), white text has approximately 1.9:1 contrast ratio, failing WCAG AA requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1386: Use a darker text color or add a semi-transparent dark overlay to ensure text contrast, or adjust the gradient to use darker colors on the right side.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed globally without sufficient alternative
issuesFound.explanation: All anchor elements have text-decoration: none by default, and many link instances (such as footer navigation links, account navigation links, and header links) rely solely on color to distinguish them from surrounding text, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 288: Remove the global text-decoration: none, or add explicit underline on hover/focus for all link contexts, or add a non-color visual differentiator such as border-bottom: 1px solid transparent; border-bottom-color: currentColor on hover.
