issuesFound.title: Broken screen-reader-only utility class removes visual hiding
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class sets position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes content visible to sighted users instead of hiding it visually while keeping it for assistive technologies. This contradicts the class name and comment, causing visual clutter and potential confusion.

issuesFound.title: Global :focus-visible removes default focus indicator without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus style (such as box-shadow or border-color change) for all interactive elements. This removes the browser's default focus ring, leaving keyboard users without visible focus indicators unless each component defines its own.

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px but does not define a visible replacement focus indicator (such as box-shadow or border-color change). This leaves buttons without any visible focus state for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button's focus-visible state sets outline: none without adding a visible alternative like box-shadow or border-color change, removing the focus indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: FAQ toggle button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ toggle button sets outline: none on focus-visible without providing a visible alternative focus indicator, making it impossible for keyboard users to see when the button has focus.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set outline: none on focus-visible without defining a visible replacement (such as border-bottom or background-color change), removing focus visibility for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Social media link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links set outline: none on focus-visible without providing a visible alternative focus indicator, leaving keyboard users without visual feedback.

issuesFound.title: Footer brand link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The footer brand link sets outline: none on focus-visible without adding a visible replacement focus indicator, making the focus state invisible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Account navigation link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links set outline: none on focus-visible without defining a visible alternative (such as box-shadow or underline), removing the focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Submenu link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links set outline: none on focus-visible without providing a visible replacement focus indicator, leaving keyboard users unable to see focus.

issuesFound.title: Header logo link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The header logo link sets outline: none on focus-visible without adding a visible alternative focus indicator, making the focused state invisible.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button sets outline: none on focus-visible without providing a visible replacement, removing the focus indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Table wrapper focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The table wrapper sets outline: none on focus-visible without defining a visible alternative, removing the focus indicator for keyboard users who tab to the scrollable region.

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. While padding increases the total height to 32px, the explicit min-height of 20px is insufficient for the interactive element itself.

issuesFound.title: Icon-only button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. While padding increases the total size, the explicit dimensions are too small.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox input touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input sets width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or dyslexia, making it harder to distinguish between words.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed globally without non-color alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Unless specific links provide non-color visual differentiators (such as border-bottom, font-weight changes, or background highlights), links rely on color alone to distinguish them from surrounding text, failing WCAG 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast for input hint text
issuesFound.explanation: The .input-hint class uses var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa against a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text, making hints difficult to read for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast for required field marker
issuesFound.explanation: The .required-marker class uses var(--colour-error) which is #ee9999 against a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.6:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast for statistics label text
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label class uses rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) (white at 30% opacity) against the gradient background (approximately #5588bb to #bb88ee). This results in a contrast ratio well below 4.5:1, making the text difficult to read for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast for pricing toggle label
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle span uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against var(--colour-bg-subtle) (#f8fafc), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient color contrast for footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links use var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against var(--colour-neutral-900) (#555555) background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.6:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Forced colors mode removes focus outline
issuesFound.explanation: The forced-colors media query sets outline: none on :focus-visible without providing a visible alternative (such as a transparent outline or border), which removes the focus indicator for users in Windows High Contrast Mode or other forced color modes.

issuesFound.title: Button transition missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transform var(--transition-fast)), which causes spatial movement animation. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform transition for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Hero image transition missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image includes transform in its transition (transform var(--transition-slower)), which creates spatial movement. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for .hero-image img is empty and does not disable the transition.

issuesFound.title: Broken screen reader utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, which keeps content in the document flow and visible, failing to visually hide content while keeping it accessible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 414-425: Replace with `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Broken screen reader focusable utility
issuesFound.explanation: .sr-only-focusable uses position: static, making content always visible rather than only when focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 427-436: Replace with `.sr-only-focusable:not(:focus):not(:focus-within) { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; } .sr-only-focusable:focus, .sr-only-focusable:focus-within { position: static; width: auto; height: auto; overflow: visible; clip: auto; white-space: normal; }`

issuesFound.title: Button transitions lack reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: .btn includes transition properties for transform (spatial movement) without a prefers-reduced-motion media query to disable or instant-complete the animation for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 865: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Hero image transitions lack reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: .hero-image img has transitions on transform and box-shadow without a proper reduced motion override (the existing media query at line 927 is empty).
issuesFound.fix: Line 927-930: Add inside the media query `transition: none;` and `transform: none;` for both the base and hover states.

issuesFound.title: Feature card transitions lack reduced motion override
issuesFound.explanation: Feature cards have transform transitions without a reduced motion override that disables the transition itself (the media query only resets the transform value on hover).
issuesFound.fix: Add to the media query at line 1010: `.feature-card { transition: none; }`

issuesFound.title: Product card transitions lack reduced motion override

issuesFound.title: Stat item transitions lack reduced motion override

issuesFound.title: Pricing card transitions lack reduced motion override

issuesFound.title: Skip link transitions lack reduced motion override

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words and impairs readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping but never instantiates createFocusTrap or activates it. When the mobile menu opens, keyboard focus is not confined to the drawer, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay into the underlying page content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2109: Add _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); 
  Line 2110: Add _trap.activate();

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but does not update aria-expanded when the suggestion list opens or closes. Screen readers cannot announce whether the listbox popup is visible or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2814: Add setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); inside _open() after _searchState.open = true;
  Line 2827: Add setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); inside _close() after _searchState.open = false;

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The combobox input is created with aria-haspopup and aria-controls, but lacks the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute required by the ARIA combobox pattern. Assistive technologies assume the popup is expanded by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2893: Add setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); after mounting the listbox in the init function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering dropdown triggers, the code sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls, but fails to initialise aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Assistive technologies assume the menu is expanded by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2330: Add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); after setting aria-controls in the register function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the navigation menu opens or closes, no announcement is made to screen readers. Users navigating with screen readers are not informed that the menu state has changed or that they are now inside a navigation menu.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2112: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened'); inside open() function.
  Line 2138: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); inside close() function.

issuesFound.title: pricing price updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, the price values update visually with animation, but the new prices are not announced to screen readers. Users relying on audio feedback are not informed of the price change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3195: Add liveRegion.announce(`Price updated to ${target} for ${plan.id} plan`); after updating the price display text.

issuesFound.title: productFilter results not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count updates, but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users are not informed how many products match the selected criteria or if the filter resulted in zero products.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2645: Add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`); inside the filterAnimation.run callback after _updateCountBadge.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function not updating aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens the mobile navigation drawer but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen readers will not announce the expanded state of the navigation menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line: function open(announceToSR) { ... emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer }); setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); }

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close function not updating aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the mobile navigation drawer but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. This leaves screen readers with stale state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line: function close(announceToSR) { ... emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer }); setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); }

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" on page load, violating the requirement that the initial state of expandable content must be programmatically determinable.
issuesFound.fix: Line: _trigger.addEventListener('click', _onTriggerClick); setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function not updating aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens, the function does not update aria-expanded to "true" on the trigger element, so assistive technologies cannot convey the open state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: function _openInstance(inst) { if (inst.open) return; inst.open = true; setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'); ... }

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close function not updating aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu closes, the function does not update aria-expanded to "false" on the trigger element.
issuesFound.fix: Line: function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus) { if (!inst.open) return; inst.open = false; setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); ... }

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are initialized with aria-haspopup and aria-controls, but aria-expanded is not set to "false" initially.
issuesFound.fix: Line: if (!trigger.getAttribute('aria-controls')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId); setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function not updating aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item opens, the function fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (item.trigger), preventing screen readers from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: function open(item, announceToSR) { if (!isPlainObject(item) || item.open) return; item.open = true; setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); addClass(item.el, CSS.open); ... }

issuesFound.title: Search combobox open function not updating aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When the search suggestion panel opens, the combobox input does not have aria-expanded updated to "true", failing to communicate the popup visibility to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line: function _open() { if (_searchState.open) return; _searchState.open = true; setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); ... }

issuesFound.title: Search combobox close function not updating aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When the search suggestion panel closes, aria-expanded is not set to "false" on the combobox input.
issuesFound.fix: Line: function _close() { if (!_searchState.open) return; _searchState.open = false; setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); ... }

issuesFound.title: Search combobox missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads.
issuesFound.fix: Line: setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox'); setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period buttons (monthly/annual/quarterly) function as toggle buttons but do not set aria-pressed to reflect the active state, leaving screen reader users unaware which period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line: _periodBtns.forEach(btn => { const btnPeriod = btn.getAttribute('data-period'); const isActive = btnPeriod === period; toggleAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, true, false, isActive); toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); });

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs missing aria-selected state
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons implement a roving tabindex pattern but do not set aria-selected="true" on the active tab or "false" on inactive tabs, preventing screen readers from conveying the current selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line: qsa('[data-filter-value]', _tabsEl).forEach(btn => { const isActive = btn.getAttribute('data-filter-value') === _productFilterState.activeCategory; setAttr(btn, ARIA.SELECTED, isActive); toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); });

issuesFound.title: Form validation not setting aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation marks a field as invalid, the function adds aria-describedby pointing to the error message but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself, which is required for screen readers to identify invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line: function markInvalid(field, errorEl) { if (!isElement(field) || !isElement(errorEl)) return; setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true); addDescribedBy(field, errorEl); }

issuesFound.title: Form validation not clearing aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the function removes the error from aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="false" (or remove the attribute), leaving stale invalid state on the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line: function markValid(field, errorEl) { if (!isElement(field)) return; setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false); if (isElement(errorEl) && errorEl.id) { ... } }

issuesFound.title: Pricing module not announcing price changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch billing periods, the price values update visually but no announcement is made to screen readers via a live region, meaning users may not be aware the prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period }); liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || `Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`); }

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens without activating a focus trap (_trap is declared but never initialized or activated), allowing keyboard focus to escape to background content while the drawer is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line: function open(announceToSR) { ... _open = true; if (!_trap) { _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { initialFocus: _drawer, returnFocus: _trigger }); } _trap.activate(); emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer }); }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initialises, it does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button. Assistive technologies will not know the initial collapsed state of the menu.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels by removing/adding the `hidden` attribute and toggling CSS classes, but it never sets `aria-expanded` on the trigger element (`inst.trigger`). This prevents screen readers from announcing the state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1669: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside `_openInstance`. Line 1687: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside `_closeInstance`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: During initialisation, the dropdown menu registers triggers and panels but does not initialise the `aria-expanded` attribute to `"false"` for closed dropdowns.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1765 (inside register, or in init after registration): Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when the panel is initially hidden.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is opened, the code sets `item.open = true` and updates CSS classes, but it only sets `aria-expanded="false"` in the `close` function (line 3197). The `open` function lacks the corresponding `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`, leaving screen readers unaware that the item is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3170 (inside open): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox (`role="combobox"`) that shows/hides a suggestion listbox. The `_open` and `_close` functions manage the internal state and UI visibility but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the input element to reflect whether the suggestions are visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2286: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside `_open`. Line 2297: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside `_close`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is assigned `role="combobox"` and `aria-haspopup="listbox"`, but `aria-expanded` is never initialised to `"false"` for the closed state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2372 (inside init, after setting role and haspopup): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed (or aria-selected)
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period buttons (monthly/quarterly/yearly) function as a toggle group or tab list. The code toggles CSS active classes but does not set `aria-pressed` (if buttons) or `aria-selected` (if tabs) to indicate the selected state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2728: Inside the loop, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` (or `aria-selected` if implementing as tabs).

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing toggle switch (`_toggleEl`) is assigned `role="switch"` (line 2810) but the code never sets `aria-checked` to reflect the on/off state (annual vs monthly). A switch role requires `aria-checked`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2812 (in init): Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));`. Line 2745 (in _onToggleClick): Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(next === 'year'));` after calculating `next`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is marked invalid, the function updates `aria-describedby` to link the error message but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the control. Assistive technologies will not know the field has an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1287: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is marked valid, the function removes the error description association but does not remove `aria-invalid` or set it to `"false"`. The field remains marked as invalid to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1304: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` (or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`) after clearing `aria-describedby`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens or closes, no announcement is made to screen readers via the `liveRegion` utility. Users navigating with screen readers may not realise the menu has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1533 (in open): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`. Line 1553 (in close): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown menu updates the visual UI but does not announce the change to screen readers using the `liveRegion` utility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1674 (in _openInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');`. Line 1693 (in _closeInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items does not trigger a live region announcement. While the `aria-expanded` fix (Issue 5) helps, an explicit announcement improves the user experience for dynamic content changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3178 (in open): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.title) || '${item.title} expanded');`. Line 3209 (in close): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.title) || '${item.title} collapsed');`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates prices visually and via text content, but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not notice the price values have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2703 (in setPeriod): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing');` (and similar for monthly).

issuesFound.title: productFilter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product grid updates visually with animations, but the change in result count is not announced to screen readers. The `_updateCountBadge` updates a visual badge only.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2466 (inside applyFilters, in the onDone callback): Add `liveRegion.announce('${totalMatches} products found');`.

issuesFound.title: breadcrumbHelpers incomplete implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The function contains a comment `// Mark the last item as the current page.` but the actual code to set `aria-current="page"` on the last link or span is missing. The function ends without fulfilling its documented accessibility requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1938 (before the comment about separators): Add code to find the last link or span in the breadcrumb and call `setAttr(lastLinkOrSpan, 'aria-current', 'page');`.

issuesFound.explanation: The current page link is styled visually but lacks aria-current="page", so screen readers cannot identify it as the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line: `<a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>` change to `<a href={item.href} className={...} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link lacks aria-current="page", preventing screen readers from announcing the current location.
issuesFound.fix: Line: `<a href={item.href} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>` change to `<a href={item.href} className={...} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} onClick={onClose}>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop, but none is provided, resulting in an icon-only button with no accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line: `<IconButton onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">✕</IconButton>` change to `<IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">✕</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton lacks an aria-label prop, so the theme toggle button has no accessible name for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line: `<IconButton onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">` change to `<IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous IconButton missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next IconButton missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel non-visible slide missing aria-hidden

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without visible replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing any visible alternative focus indicator such as box-shadow or a distinct border. Keyboard-only users will have no visual indication of which element is focused, making navigation impossible for sighted keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 248-251: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) rule sets outline: none on :focus-visible without providing any visible alternative. In Windows High Contrast mode, users will have no visible focus indicator at all.
issuesFound.fix: Line 258-261: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid CanvasText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class exposes content instead of hiding it
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto — these properties make content VISIBLE rather than hiding it. This is the opposite of the intended purpose. Screen-reader-only content will be visible to all users, and content meant to be hidden will not be hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 276-285: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on stat labels
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label uses color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) which is white at 30% opacity. Against the gradient background (#5588bb to #bb88ee defined at lines 9-10), the blended foreground colour results in approximately 1.5:1 contrast ratio — far below the required 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on newsletter section paragraph
issuesFound.explanation: The .newsletter-section > p uses color: rgba(255,255,255,0.8) which is white at 80% opacity. Against the gradient background (#5588bb to #bb88ee), the blended foreground colour results in approximately 2.5:1 contrast ratio — below the required 4.5:1 for 16px text.

issuesFound.title: Root font-size set below recommended minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px, which is below the recommended 16px (1rem) baseline. This affects all text that uses rem units, making body text smaller than recommended for comfortable reading.
issuesFound.fix: Line 91: font-size: 1rem;

issuesFound.title: Paragraph line-height below recommended minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The p element sets line-height: 1, which is below the recommended 1.5 for body text. Single line-height makes text blocks difficult to read, especially for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 140: line-height: var(--line-height-relaxed);

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, a negative value that compresses word spacing. This reduces readability for all users and can cause particular difficulty for those with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 96: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets letter-spacing: -0.05em, a negative value that compresses character spacing. This reduces readability for all users and can cause particular difficulty for those with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 97: letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal);

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without alternative visual distinction
issuesFound.explanation: The a element sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links globally. Without underlines, links are distinguished from surrounding text by colour alone, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color for users who cannot perceive colour differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 115: a { text-decoration: underline; } and add specific overrides for navigation links: .nav a, footer a { text-decoration: none; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 24×24 CSS pixels. Users with motor impairments or touch input will have difficulty activating these buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, both below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 24×24 CSS pixels. Icon-only buttons are particularly difficult to activate with touch when undersized.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] sets width: 20px and height: 20px, below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 24×24 CSS pixels. Checkboxes are difficult to activate with touch when undersized.

issuesFound.title: Empty prefers-reduced-motion media query provides no override
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty, providing no actual motion reduction. The comment claims it suppresses transitions and animations, but the empty block does nothing. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience all animations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 265-267: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; scroll-behavior: auto !important; } }

issuesFound.title: Transform transition on .btn missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transform var(--transition-fast)), and .btn:active applies transform: translateY(1px). There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule that disables this transform animation for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 306: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Transform transition on .skip-link missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class has transition: transform var(--transition-base) for the slide-in animation. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule that disables this transform animation.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 295: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transform: translateY(-200%); transition: none; } .skip-link:focus { transform: translateY(0); } }

issuesFound.title: Transform transition on hero image with empty reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has transform transitions, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block contains empty rules that do not actually disable the transform. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience the 3D rotation effect on hover.
issuesFound.fix: Line 590-593: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Transform transition on hero CTA buttons missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary includes transform in its transition and applies transform: translateY(-1px) on hover. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule that disables this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 568: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

fpTitles: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
Transform transition on hero image with empty reduced-motion override

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class is broken — content is visible instead of hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto — these properties make content fully visible. This class is intended to hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers, but the current implementation does the opposite, potentially exposing internal labels or skip links unintentionally.

issuesFound.title: Root font-size set to 12px — below recommended minimum for body text
issuesFound.explanation: Setting html { font-size: 12px } establishes a base font size below the recommended 16px (1rem default). This affects all rem-based sizing throughout the stylesheet, making body text smaller than optimal for readability, particularly for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing on body impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has word-spacing: -0.2em which reduces spacing between words. Negative word-spacing can make text harder to read, especially for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, as words may appear to run together.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing on body impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has letter-spacing: -0.05em which tightens character spacing. Negative letter-spacing reduces readability for users with low vision or reading disabilities, as characters may appear cramped.

issuesFound.title: Paragraph line-height set to 1 — below recommended minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The p element has line-height: 1 which provides no spacing between lines. WCAG recommends a minimum line-height of 1.5 for body text to ensure readability. A line-height of 1 can cause lines of text to touch, making reading difficult for users with dyslexia or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on :focus-visible without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px and border-radius, providing no visible focus indicator. Keyboard users will have no visual indication of which element has focus, making navigation impossible for sighted keyboard-only users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .btn:focus-visible without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule removes the outline without providing an alternative visible focus indicator (no box-shadow, border change, or background change on focus). Keyboard users cannot see which button has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .nav-toggle:focus-visible without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible rule removes the outline without providing an alternative visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when the navigation toggle button has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on search submit button without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible rule removes outline without providing an alternative visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when the search button has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on account navigation links without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible rule removes outline without providing an alternative visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see which account navigation link has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on feature card links without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible rule removes outline without providing an alternative visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when "Learn more" links in feature cards have focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on filter tab buttons without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button:focus-visible rule removes outline without providing an alternative visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see which filter tab has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on pricing toggle buttons without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible rule removes outline without providing an alternative visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see which billing toggle button has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on table wrapper without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .table-wrapper:focus-visible rule removes outline without providing an alternative visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when the scrollable table container has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on FAQ accordion buttons without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible rule removes outline and uses outline-offset: -3px without providing an alternative visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see which FAQ question has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on checkbox inputs without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible rule removes outline without providing an alternative visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see which checkbox has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on footer brand links without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand a:focus-visible rule removes outline without providing an alternative visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see when footer brand links have focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on footer navigation links without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible rule removes outline without providing an alternative visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see which footer navigation link has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on social links without visible replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a:focus-visible rule removes outline without providing an alternative visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see which social media link has focus.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size — .btn-sm has min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum touch target size of 24px. Users with motor impairments or touch devices may have difficulty activating these buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size — .btn-icon has min-width and min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px which are below the WCAG 2.2 minimum touch target size of 24px. Icon-only buttons may be difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size — checkbox inputs are 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] rule sets width: 20px and height: 20px which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum touch target size of 24px. Checkboxes may be difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Empty prefers-reduced-motion media query provides no override
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty, providing no actual overrides for users who prefer reduced motion. Animations and transitions defined elsewhere (such as .skip-link transform transition) will still play, potentially causing vestibular disorders or motion sensitivity issues.

issuesFound.title: Button transform transition missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class has a transform transition that causes spatial movement on :active state (transform: translateY(1px)), but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this motion for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform transition has empty reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has a transform transition for 3D perspective effects, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 686-689 contains empty rules and does not actually disable the transition or transform.

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed globally — color alone distinguishes links
issuesFound.explanation: The rule a { text-decoration: none; } removes underlines from all links globally. Without underlines, links are distinguished from surrounding text by color alone, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color. Users with color vision deficiencies may not be able to identify links.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) rule for :focus-visible removes the outline without providing an alternative visible focus indicator. In Windows High Contrast Mode, users will have no visible indication of keyboard focus, making navigation impossible for sighted keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not create or activate focus trap for drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares `_trap = null` but never assigns a focus trap instance. When the drawer opens, focus is not moved into it, keyboard focus is not trapped within it, and focus is not returned to the trigger on close. Keyboard users can Tab out of the drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2858: `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { initialFocus: _drawer }); _trap.activate();` and Line 2878: `if (_trap) { _trap.deactivate(); _trap = null; }`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() does not set aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When closing a dropdown, the function removes CSS classes and sets hidden but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2992: `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function sets item.open = true and adds CSS classes but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an asymmetry.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3817: `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function links the field to the error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen reader users are not notified that the field has an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1973: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The function removes the error message reference from aria-describedby but never clears aria-invalid. The field remains marked as invalid even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1985: `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is never initialized to "false" on page load. Screen readers may report an unknown expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2920: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register() does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown, aria-haspopup is set but aria-expanded is never initialized to "false". Screen readers may report an unknown expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3050: `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle with role="switch" does not set aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: When the toggle element is not a native button, role="switch" is applied, but aria-checked is never set. For role="switch", aria-checked (not aria-pressed) is the required state attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4047: `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(isYear));`

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the visible product count changes after filtering, the count badge text updates but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed of the result count change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4190: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(count) || \`Showing \${count} products\`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, prices update visually but no live region announcement informs screen reader users of the change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4019: `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? (STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing') : (STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing'));`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item opens or closes, no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3835: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question || ''));` and Line 3855: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question || ''));`

issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded initialized to "false" on page load. Assistive technologies cannot announce the initial collapsed state.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register() does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator._renderResult() does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field fails validation, the function adds error CSS classes and shows an error message but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen reader users cannot programmatically determine the field's invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5300: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator._renderResult() does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field passes validation, the function removes error CSS classes but never clears aria-invalid (sets it to "false" or removes it). Screen reader users may still hear the field announced as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5300: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters() does not announce filter results to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When products are filtered, the count badge updates visually but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed about how many products match the current filter.

issuesFound.title: pricing._updateSavingsMessage() updates content without live region
issuesFound.explanation: The savings message element is updated dynamically when billing period changes but lacks aria-live attribute. Screen reader users are not informed about the savings percentage change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4480: `_savingsMsg.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ accordion item is opened, the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button is not set to "true". Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware that content has been revealed. The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded to "false", but open() omits setting it to "true".

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the aria-expanded attribute on the hamburger button (navToggle) is not set to "true". Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation has expanded. The close() function similarly lacks the aria-expanded update.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register() does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown trigger is registered, aria-expanded is not initialized to "false". The panel is hidden (hidden attribute set), but the trigger lacks aria-expanded="false", so screen readers have no indication of the initial collapsed state.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid inputs
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation marks a field as invalid, aria-invalid is not set to "true". Screen readers will not announce the invalid state, making it difficult for users to identify which fields have errors.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid on valid inputs
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation clears an error, aria-invalid is not reset to "false" or removed. Screen readers may continue announcing the field as invalid even after the error is corrected.

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod() does not announce billing period change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled (monthly/annual), the visible price changes but no live region announcement informs screen reader users of the change. Users may not realize the prices have updated.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not manage focus into the navigation drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus is not moved into the drawer. Keyboard users must manually tab to the navigation, and screen reader users are not positioned at the newly revealed content. The module declares a `_trap` variable but never activates a focus trap.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron span missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron arrow characters (▲/▼) inside the NavDropdown button are decorative indicators of the dropdown state, but they are not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers may announce "up-pointing triangle" or "down-pointing triangle" in addition to the button's text label, creating redundant or confusing output.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~477: {isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}

issuesFound.title: HeroSection missing aria-labelledby for landmark
issuesFound.explanation: The HeroSection contains a heading with id="hero-heading" but the section element does not reference it via aria-labelledby. When multiple sections exist on a page, each should have an accessible name to allow screen reader users to navigate by landmark and understand the section's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~640: <section className="hero-section" aria-labelledby="hero-heading">

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing aria-labelledby for landmark
issuesFound.explanation: The StatsBar section contains a heading with id="stats-heading" but the section element does not reference it via aria-labelledby. Without an accessible name, screen reader users navigating by landmarks cannot distinguish this section from other unnamed sections on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~710: <section className="stats-bar" aria-labelledby="stats-heading">

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker asterisk missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk character (*) used to indicate required fields is visible text that will be announced by screen readers as "asterisk" in addition to the VisuallyHidden "(required)" text. This creates redundant and potentially confusing announcements for users of assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~352: *

issuesFound.title: Button spinner missing aria-hidden during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The btn-spinner span is a decorative loading indicator that provides no meaningful information beyond what the VisuallyHidden loading label already announces. Without aria-hidden, some screen readers may attempt to describe the spinner element, creating redundant output.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~295: <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation link for the current page uses a CSS class ('nav-link--current') to indicate visually that it represents the current page, but this information is not exposed to assistive technology. Screen reader users cannot identify which page they are currently on. Use aria-current="page" to programmatically indicate the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~510: <a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current page link missing aria-current

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute placed on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the modal-overlay div, but this element has no role. The aria-modal attribute must be placed on the element with role="dialog" (the modal-dialog div) to properly communicate to screen readers that content outside the dialog is inert.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1350: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>
  Fix: Line ~1352: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: StarRating star characters missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The star characters (★/☆) inside the StarRating component are decorative visual representations of the rating value. The outer span already provides an aria-label with the full rating text. Without aria-hidden on the inner content, screen readers may announce the star characters in addition to the aria-label, creating redundant output.

  htmlFor="contact-name"
  required
  errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}
  errorId="contact-name-error"
>
  <TextInput
    id="contact-name"
    name="name"
    type="text"
    autoComplete="name"
    required
    aria-required="true"
    value={name.state.value}
    onChange={(ev) => name.onChange(ev.target.value)}
    onBlur={name.onBlur}
    invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error}
    aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name-error' : undefined}
  />
</FormGroup>

(Similar fixes needed for email, subject, and message fields with corresponding errorId values: contact-email-error, contact-subject-error, contact-message-error)
